ocr.text: In the context of evaluating the effects of various treatments on biological responses, the data presented in the accompanying
table offers a comprehensive overview of the relationship between dosage and the resultant inhibition of writhing responses.
The table categorizes different groups based on the administered doses (mg/kg), highlighting the correlation between increasing
doses and the corresponding number of writhing responses observed. For instance, the control group, receiving 1% Tween 80 in
water at a dose of 10 mL/kg, exhibited an average of 8.8 ± 0.374 writhing responses, underscoring the baseline activity against
which other treatments can be measured.

Control  1% Tween 80 in water (10 mL/kg)  8.8 ± 0.374  —

Leaf litter  3,055 (±181):43 (±1):1  10  73  7  Yuan and Chen (2009)
Soil organic matter  287 (±25):17 (±1):1  2  7  3  Xu et al. (2013)
Soil microbes  42 (±4):6 (±0.4):1  Xu et al. (2013)

Additionally, the metric of inhibition (%) provides insight into the efficacy of the treatments in reducing these responses, a
crucial aspect for determining the potential therapeutic value of the tested substances. Furthermore, the table includes detailed
information on organic material and molar ratios, such as C:N:P, which are essential for understanding the underlying
biochemical interactions and their implications on the observed biological effects. This data serves as a valuable resource for
researchers aiming to explore these dynamics further.
